metadata.title: States PCS: Karnataka GK for KPSC and other Karnataka State Exams

questions.question: Which industry is the largest consumer of dolomite?
questions.explanation: The iron and steel industry consumes over 90% of dolomite. Other users include the fertilizer industry (4%), ferro-alloys and glass industries (2% each), alloy steel (1%), and others (1%).

questions.question: In which Karnataka district is the Nagjhari Hydro Power Plant located?
questions.explanation: Nagjhari Hydro Power Plant is situated in Ambikanagar Village, Haliyal Taluk of Uttara Kannada district in Karnataka. Uttara Kannada is a coastal district known for its forests and rivers, making it suitable for hydro power projects like Nagjhari. The plant harnesses river water in this region for electricity generation.

questions.question: Kadra Dam is located in which Karnataka district?
questions.explanation: Kadra Dam is situated in Uttara Kannada district of Karnataka. The hydroelectric project began generating power in 1997, contributing to the region’s energy supply. Uttara Kannada is known for its dense forests and river systems, making it suitable for such dam projects.

questions.question: Karnataka was formed as Mysore State on:
questions.explanation: Karnataka was created by the States Reorganization Act, 1956 as Mysore State on 1 November 1956 when several Indian states were created by redrawing borders based on linguistic demographics.

questions.question: At which place is the Suvarna Vidhana Soudha located?
questions.explanation: The Suvarna Vidhana Soudha is the legislature building of the State of Karnataka in Belgaum in the Belgaum district of Northern Karnataka. It was inaugurated on 11 October 2012 by President Pranab Mukherjee.

questions.question: The committee which recommended a three-tier Panchayati Raj System in India was:
questions.explanation: Balwant Rai Mehta Committee was constituted in 1957. This committee recommended three-tier local self government under Panchayati Raj System.

questions.question: The Research Centre for DNA Diagnostics is located in which of the following districts?
questions.explanation: The Research Centre for DNA Diagnostics (RCDD) is a medical research facility at Karnatak University, Dharwar, Karnataka. The primary objective of the centre is to identify hereditary disorders prevalent in north Karnataka and to conduct advanced research.

questions.question: Which district in Karnataka has highest number of Waterfalls?
questions.explanation: Shivamogga district in Karnataka has highest number of Waterfalls.

questions.question: Which of the following places is known for Karigudda and Biligudda?
questions.explanation: Savandurga is best known for Karigudda and Biligudda. These literally mean the “black hill” and the “white hill”. Karigudda and Biligudda rise to a height of 1226 metres above the Deccan Plateau.

questions.question: At which of the following places Bedara Vesha dance is famous?
questions.explanation: Bedara Vesha is a folk dance performed days before Holi night in Sirsi town of Karnataka. People of Sirsi celebrate Holi with this unique folk dance every alternate year.
